title: overview of Brazil Programa Remessa Conforme - WCO
extracted:
### Key Dates
- Launched: August 2023
- First international shipments arrived: End of September 2023
- Operational period analyzed: August to December 2023
- Procomex E-Commerce Study Group created: 2020
- Series of 31 meetings: November 2021 to October 2023 (13 involving Receita Federal do Brasil (RFB) and Customs Department)
- Comprehensive report delivered to RFB: November 2022

### Key Statistics (Pre-PRC Context, 2020-2022)
- Imported packages increase: 338.52% (from 52,779,466 in 2020 to 178,666,498 in 2022)
- E-commerce Shipment Import Declarations (DIR) increase: 201.93% (from 2,805,702 in 2020 to 5,665,605 in 2022)
- Shipments with registered declarations: 5.31% in 2020; 3.17% in 2022
- Brazil’s postal operator share of e-commerce shipments: >95%
- Estimated uncollected ICMS and import duties (2020-2022): >US$ 4.2 billion (R$ 21.1 billion)
- Flat duty rate: 60% for goods via Simplified Customs Clearance
- Gifts <US$50: Exempt from duties; only 2 states collecting ICMS

### Program Criteria and Benefits
- Open to: National and foreign companies using e-commerce platforms, websites, or digital tools
- Certified companies: Few companies responsible for major portion of shipments (exact number not stated)
- Postal deliveries of all e-commerce shipments (Aug-Dec 2023): 99.05%
- Postal deliveries of PRC DIR (Aug-Dec 2023): 99.47%
- Procomex E-Commerce Study Group: 28 organizations representing >80% of market

### Tax Collection Impacts (Aug-Dec 2023 vs. Same Period 2022)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total collected increase | 222.91% higher |
| Revenue increase | R$ 386 million (US$ 77 million) |

**Note**: Postal delivery comparative table for 2022 and 2023 (Aug-Dec, before/after PRC, emphasizing Registered DIRs) referenced but data not explicitly detailed in page. Express/courier shipments excluded from table. No specific participation numbers (e.g., total certified companies) stated beyond "a few companies, responsible for a major portion of shipments." Zero percent import duty for shipments ≤US$50 to non-commercial consumers; prioritized clearance and reduced inspection rate offered.
